annotations.label: Mathematical knot

anchor.snippet: a knot is an embedding of a circle in 3-dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: Mathlib has generic topological embeddings (Topology.IsEmbedding) and Euclidean spheres but no definition of a knot as an embedded circle in ℝ³.

annotations.label: Higher-dimensional knot

anchor.snippet: a higher-dimensional knot is an n -dimensional sphere embedded in

annotations.note: No notion of an embedded n-sphere knot exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Knot as a simple closed curve

anchor.snippet: A knot is created by beginning with a one- dimensional line segment, wrapping it around itself arbitrarily

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define knots or closed-curve constructions of them.

annotations.label: Knot equivalence (orientation-preserving homeomorphism)

anchor.snippet: are equivalent if there is an orientation-preserving homeomorphism

annotations.note: Homeomorph exists but there is no formalized notion of knot equivalence.

annotations.label: Ambient isotopy

anchor.snippet: there is a continuous family of homeomorphisms

annotations.note: Mathlib has homotopies of maps but no definition of ambient isotopy (isotopy appears only as a docstring mention).

annotations.label: Equivalence of the two notions of knot equivalence

anchor.snippet: These two notions of knot equivalence agree exactly about which knots are equivalent

annotations.note: Neither notion of knot equivalence is formalized, so their agreement is absent.

annotations.label: Isotopy makes every knot trivial

anchor.snippet: this definition would make every knot equivalent to the unknot

annotations.note: No formalization of knots, isotopy, or the unknot exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Recognition problem

anchor.snippet: the recognition problem , is determining the equivalence of two knots

annotations.note: The knot recognition problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Knot diagram

anchor.snippet: The resulting diagram is an immersed plane curve with the additional data

annotations.note: Knot diagrams (immersed plane curves with crossing data) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reduced diagram

anchor.snippet: A reduced diagram is a knot diagram in which there are no reducible crossings

annotations.note: No knot diagram infrastructure exists, hence no reduced-diagram notion.

annotations.label: Petal projection

anchor.snippet: A petal projection is a type of projection in which

annotations.note: Petal projections are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reidemeister moves theorem

anchor.snippet: two knot diagrams belonging to the same knot can be related by a sequence of three kinds of moves

annotations.note: Reidemeister moves are only mentioned in the Quandle docstring, not formalized as a theorem.

annotations.label: Knot invariant

anchor.snippet: that is the same for equivalent knots

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes quandles/racks as algebraic knot invariants, but not the general definition of a knot invariant.

annotations.label: Tricolorability (elementary invariant)

anchor.snippet: An elementary invariant is tricolorability

annotations.note: Tricolorability corresponds to quandle colorings, and quandles exist in Mathlib, but tricolorability itself is not defined.

annotations.label: Knot group and Alexander polynomial

anchor.snippet: knot invariants include the knot group , which is the fundamental group of the knot complement

annotations.note: The fundamental group is formalized, but the knot complement, knot group, and Alexander polynomial are not.

annotations.label: Knot polynomial

anchor.snippet: A knot polynomial is a knot invariant that is a polynomial

annotations.note: Mathlib has Polynomial but no notion of a knot polynomial.

annotations.label: Alexander–Conway polynomial

anchor.snippet: the Alexander–Conway polynomial , is a polynomial in the variable z with integer coefficients

annotations.note: The Alexander–Conway polynomial is not formalized (matches for "Alexander" are Alexander's subbasis theorem in topology).

annotations.label: Recursive (skein) definition of Alexander–Conway polynomial

anchor.snippet: is recursively defined according to the rules

annotations.note: No recursive/skein definition of the Alexander–Conway polynomial exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Skein relation

anchor.snippet: The second rule is what is often referred to as a skein relation

annotations.note: Skein relations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Alexander–Conway polynomial of the trefoil

anchor.snippet: It computes the Alexander–Conway polynomial of the trefoil knot

annotations.note: Neither the trefoil knot nor knot polynomials are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trefoil is not the unknot

anchor.snippet: this shows that the trefoil is not equivalent to the unknot

annotations.note: Trefoil and unknot are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trefoils are not amphichiral

anchor.snippet: meaning that they are not amphichiral

annotations.note: Amphichirality and the trefoil are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jones polynomial distinguishes the trefoils

anchor.snippet: The Jones polynomial can in fact distinguish between the left- and right-handed trefoil knots

annotations.note: The Jones polynomial is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Thurston: many knots are hyperbolic

anchor.snippet: William Thurston proved many knots are hyperbolic knots

annotations.note: Hyperbolic knots and Thurston's theorem are not formalized (Mathlib "hyperbolic" matches refer to hyperbolic functions).

annotations.label: Hyperbolic structure yields knot invariants

anchor.snippet: any quantity computed from the hyperbolic structure is then a knot invariant

annotations.note: Hyperbolic structures on knot complements are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic invariants

anchor.snippet: Other hyperbolic invariants include the shape of the fundamental parallelogram, length of shortest geodesic, and volume

annotations.note: Hyperbolic knot invariants (cusp shape, geodesic length, volume) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Knots untie in four dimensions

anchor.snippet: A knot in three dimensions can be untied when placed in four-dimensional space

annotations.note: Untying of knots in 4D is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every 4D loop is the unknot

anchor.snippet: in four dimensions, any non-intersecting closed loop of one-dimensional string is equivalent to an unknot

annotations.note: This 4-dimensional triviality result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Slice-ribbon problem

anchor.snippet: asks whether every slice knot is also ribbon

annotations.note: Slice and ribbon knots, and the slice-ribbon conjecture, are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smoothly slice knot

anchor.snippet: A knot is considered smoothly slice if it can be the boundary of a disk that is smoothly embedded in a four-dimensional ball

annotations.note: Smoothly slice knots are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ribbon knot

anchor.snippet: A ribbon knot is one that bounds a disk D immersed in the 3-sphere

annotations.note: Ribbon knots are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ribbon knots are slice

anchor.snippet: All ribbon knots are known to be slice knots

annotations.note: Neither ribbon nor slice knots are formalized, so this implication is absent.

annotations.label: Knotted higher-dimensional sphere

anchor.snippet: Such an embedding is knotted if there is no homeomorphism of

annotations.note: Knottedness of higher-dimensional sphere embeddings is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General position unknotting

anchor.snippet: if m is large enough (depending on n ), the sphere should be unknotted

annotations.note: General-position unknotting in high codimension is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PL spheres knot only in codimension two

anchor.snippet: piecewise-linear n -spheres form knots only in ( n + 2)-dimensional space

annotations.note: PL knotting in codimension two is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Codimension of smooth knots can be large

anchor.snippet: Thus the codimension of a smooth knot can be arbitrarily large when not fixing the dimension

annotations.note: Smooth knotting in arbitrary codimension is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Akbulut–King: all knots are algebraic

anchor.snippet: is the link of a real-algebraic set with isolated singularity

annotations.note: The Akbulut–King theorem on knots as links of real-algebraic singularities is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-knot and n-link

anchor.snippet: An n -knot is a single

annotations.note: n-knots and n-links are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Knot sum

anchor.snippet: The operation is called the knot sum , or sometimes the connected sum or composition of two knots

annotations.note: The connected sum of knots is not formalized (the only "sum" connected-sum match is for SimpleGraphs).

annotations.label: Knot sum is commutative and associative

anchor.snippet: The knot sum of oriented knots is commutative and associative

annotations.note: Knot sum is not defined, so its commutativity/associativity is absent in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Prime knot

anchor.snippet: A knot is prime if it is non-trivial and cannot be written as the knot sum of two non-trivial knots

annotations.note: Prime knots are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Composite knot

anchor.snippet: A knot that can be written as such a sum is composite

annotations.note: Composite knots are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unique prime decomposition of knots

anchor.snippet: There is a prime decomposition for knots, analogous to prime and composite numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has unique factorization for monoids but no prime decomposition for knots (which are not defined).

annotations.label: Multiplication (bracket) of knots

anchor.snippet: introduced a binary operation on the set of oriented knot isotopy classes

annotations.note: The bracket multiplication on knot isotopy classes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Properties of the bracket operation

anchor.snippet: The operation is antisymmetric, satisfying

annotations.note: Properties of the knot bracket operation are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bracket yields trivial Alexander–Conway polynomial

anchor.snippet: is that it produces knots with a trivial Alexander–Conway polynomial

annotations.note: Neither the bracket operation nor the Alexander–Conway polynomial is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Monotonicity of prime-knot counts is open

anchor.snippet: it has not been proven that this sequence is strictly increasing

annotations.note: Counts of prime knots by crossing number are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Alexander–Briggs notation

anchor.snippet: The notation simply organizes knots by their crossing number

annotations.note: Alexander–Briggs knot notation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dowker–Thistlethwaite notation

anchor.snippet: for a knot is a finite sequence of even integers

annotations.note: Dowker–Thistlethwaite notation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conway notation

anchor.snippet: The Conway notation for knots and links, named after John Horton Conway , is based on the theory of tangles

annotations.note: Conway tangle notation for knots is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conway notation example 1*2 −3 2

anchor.snippet: An example is 1*2 −3 2

annotations.note: Conway notation and its examples are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauss code

anchor.snippet: represents a knot with a sequence of integers

annotations.note: Gauss codes for knots are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bondles (bonded quandles)

anchor.snippet: as invariants to classify self-interacting open chains

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes quandles (the basis for bondles), but bonded quandles (bondles) themselves are not defined.
